subject: Re: job opportunities
body:
of course... tell Barry anytime

--- Jen Doyle <jennifercdoyle@yahoo.com> wrote:

> Hi Jeffrey,
>    
>   As I imagine G has relayed to you, I have decided
> to begin pursuing some other job opportunities.   As
> such, I welcome all the help that you and she have
> offered - and, especially where it concerns my
> interest in pursuing something in the music
> industry, I'd really like to take you both up on it.
>  
>    
>   In the meantime, I have been looking into a couple
> of other opportunities, on my own.  I have been on
> some interviews, but at this point, all of my
> potential new employers, know both you and G, in
> some capacity.  ...Thus, it would be a tremendous
> conflict of interest for me or for them, to proceed
> any further, without first consulting you. 
> Furthermore, I wouldn't dare dream of having you get
> a surprise phone call from anyone.  I'd like us all
> to be happy and on the same page, throughout.  
>    
>   As such, would you mind:  a) receiving a phone
> call or two,  if I were to prepare you who to
> expect?  b) kindly recommending me, during these
> conversations?
>    
>   In particular, I have recently interviewed with
> IAC, for a position as Barry Diller's assistant. 
> Before I meet and interview with Mr. Diller,
> himself, however, he has asked if he may call you. 
> Is this alright? 
>    
>   Thank You,
>   Jen 
> 
> 		
> ---------------------------------
> Talk is cheap. Use Yahoo! Messenger to make
> PC-to-Phone calls.  Great rates starting at
1¢/min.

body_html:
of course... tell Barry anytime

--- Jen Doyle jennifercdoyle@yahoo.com wrote:

Hi Jeffrey,

As I imagine G has relayed to you, I have decided
to begin pursuing some other job opportunities. As
such, I welcome all the help that you and she have
offered - and, especially where it concerns my
interest in pursuing something in the music
industry, I'd really like to take you both up on it.

In the meantime, I have been looking into a couple
of other opportunities, on my own. I have been on
some interviews, but at this point, all of my
potential new employers, know both you and G, in
some capacity. ...Thus, it would be a tremendous
conflict of interest for me or for them, to proceed
any further, without first consulting you.
Furthermore, I wouldn't dare dream of having you get
a surprise phone call from anyone. I'd like us all
to be happy and on the same page, throughout.

As such, would you mind: a) receiving a phone
call or two, if I were to prepare you who to
expect? b) kindly recommending me, during these
conversations?

In particular, I have recently interviewed with
IAC, for a position as Barry Diller's assistant.
Before I meet and interview with Mr. Diller,
himself, however, he has asked if he may call you.
Is this alright?

Thank You,
Jen
